claim_text: Nominal task success can hide hidden embodiment-control assumptions.
wording_allowed: In analytic simulation, nominal success can hide low robustness radius.

claim_text: BodyBreak estimates low-cost breaking embodiment-control perturbations under a fixed evaluator budget.
tested_scope: analytic candidate pool and dense audit
strongest_alternative_explanation: Random search can find lower costs in some cases.
wording_allowed: BodyBreak estimates low-cost failures under the logged budget.

claim_text: BodyShield improves seen and held-out analytic robustness after repair.
wording_allowed: BodyShield improves analytic seen and held-out perturbation buckets.

claim_text: BodyShield beats domain randomization under matched analytic budgets.
strongest_alternative_explanation: Domain randomization may improve with other hyperparameters.
wording_allowed: Under this analytic budget, BodyShield exceeds domain randomization.

claim_text: Oracle feasibility screens out impossible analytic breaking cases.
wording_allowed: Analytic oracle rows indicate feasibility for claimed simulated failures.

claim_text: Hardware claims are blocked until noise floor and verifier agreement are measured.
One public pretrained MuJoCo checkpoint benchmark is complete with scoped actuator-loss repair.
tested_scope: SB3/RL-Zoo PPO HalfCheetah-v3 checkpoint evaluated in Gymnasium HalfCheetah-v5, seeds 20-24, horizon 1000
comparison_class: external checkpoint; action-gain baseline; domain randomization; robust control; sysID
limitations: One locomotion checkpoint only; no manipulation, foundation-policy, hardware, or broad controller-family claim.
strongest_alternative_explanation: A compute-matched action-scaling or dynamics-randomization baseline may explain some or all gain.
wording_allowed: One public pretrained MuJoCo checkpoint benchmark is complete; do not claim broad trained-policy or hardware transfer.
